component.name: Ants Terminal
component.summary: Modern, themeable terminal emulator with GPU rendering and plugins

description.p: Ants Terminal is a fast, feature-rich terminal emulator written from scratch in C++20 with Qt6. It targets the capabilities modern terminal users expect — true-color themes, ligatures, hyperlinks, inline images, and a scriptable plugin system — without dragging in a web runtime.
Key features:

ul.li: Eleven built-in themes with full ANSI palette overrides, plus automatic dark/light switching that follows the system color scheme.
Optional OpenGL glyph-atlas renderer for GPU-accelerated draws; QPainter path with ligature-aware text shaping (HarfBuzz) remains the default.
Inline images via Sixel, Kitty graphics protocol, and iTerm2 OSC 1337. Image paste auto-saves to disk — optimized for LLM / coding-agent workflows.
Shell integration via OSC 133 (prompt jump, exit-status marks). OSC 8 hyperlinks, OSC 52 clipboard, OSC 9/777 desktop notifications, OSC 9;4 progress reporting.
Kitty keyboard protocol with progressive enhancement, synchronized output, SGR mouse reporting, focus reporting.
Embedded Lua 5.4 plugin system (sandboxed) with documented ants.* API. Session persistence saves scrollback across restarts.
Built-in Project Audit panel: clazy + cppcheck + semgrep + regex rules with SARIF v2.1.0 export, AI-assisted triage, and confidence scoring.
Configurable keybindings, command snippets, auto-profile rules, SSH bookmarks, and an OpenAI-compatible AI assistant dialog.

screenshot.caption: Main terminal — custom title bar, tab gradient, status bar
Review Changes dialog — per-file diff and apply flow
Project Audit panel — clazy, cppcheck, and grep findings with SARIF export

description.p: Menubar opacity — the actual root-cause fix. 0.7.25's palette + widget-local-QSS belt-and-suspenders did not in fact suspend any belt: the user reported the desktop wallpaper still showing through the menubar strip on KWin + Breeze + Qt 6.
Fixed:

ul.li: Menubar background now actually paints opaquely under WA_TranslucentBackground (OpaqueMenuBar, MainWindow::applyTheme). User report 2026-04-25: "I can clearly see my desktop background behind it." Root cause: under WA_TranslucentBackground parent + WA_OpaquePaintEvent on the menubar, none of the conventional opaque-paint paths runs reliably on every WM/style stack. autoFillBackground is suppressed by WA_OpaquePaintEvent (the contract is "the widget paints all pixels"), QPalette::Window is only consulted by autoFillBackground and inherits the suppression, and QSS QMenuBar { background-color: … } — which is supposed to draw via QStyleSheetStyle::drawControl(CE_MenuBarEmptyArea) — is silently skipped on KWin + Breeze + Qt 6 when WA_OpaquePaintEvent is set, because the QSS engine assumes the widget owns those pixels. Net effect: every safeguard 0.7.25 added was correctly installed and not painting anything; the menubar surface stayed cleared-to-transparent and the compositor showed the wallpaper through. New file src/opaquemenubar.h defines OpaqueMenuBar, a QMenuBar subclass whose paintEvent runs QPainter::fillRect(rect(), m_bg) with CompositionMode_Source before delegating to QMenuBar::paintEvent. m_menuBar is now an OpaqueMenuBar and applyTheme sets the fill colour via setBackgroundFill(theme.bgSecondary). Regression test tests/features/menubar_hover_stylesheet extended with INV-8 — three new assertions pinning the construction site, the setBackgroundFill call, and the presence of paintEvent + fillRect inside opaquemenubar.h.

description.p: Memory-DoS hardening on OSC 8 hyperlinks and Kitty APC graphics chunks, plus a menubar-opacity fix reported mid-session. Locked by a 5-invariant regression test that flips red against pre-fix source.
Security:

ul.li: OSC 8 URI cap and Kitty APC chunk-buffer cap (TerminalGrid). Two attacker-controlled accumulators used to be bounded only by the VT parser's per-envelope 10 MB ceiling. A hostile program could emit one \x1b]8;;<10MB-URI>\x07 per scrollback line and wedge tens of GB into per-row HyperlinkSpan copies, or keep sending \x1b_G...,m=1,...\x07 frames without ever closing with m=0, growing m_kittyChunkBuffer unbounded. src/terminalgrid.h now declares MAX_OSC8_URI_BYTES = 2048 and MAX_KITTY_CHUNK_BYTES = 32 MiB. Oversized OSC 8 URIs take the invalid-scheme drop path so following text prints unlinked; the APC chunk path clears and shrink_to_fits the staging buffer on cap breach so a subsequent m=0 sees an empty buffer rather than attacker-prepended garbage.

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: Menubar renders opaque under WA_TranslucentBackground (MainWindow::applyTheme). User report 2026-04-25: "the background of the menubar is transparent." The top-level QSS cascade could race with the compositor damage rect, letting whatever sat behind the window bleed through before polish. applyTheme now installs a belt-and-suspenders opaque fill on m_menuBar — QPalette::Window = theme.bgSecondary so autoFillBackground lays down an opaque fill before QSS paints, plus a widget-local setStyleSheet(QMenuBar ...) block so the menubar's own style engine polishes independently of the top-level cascade. Mirrors the pattern already used on the custom title bar and status bar.

description.p: Wide-char (CJK / wide emoji) overwrite no longer strands its mate. 0.7.12 Tier 2 hardening item. Locked by a 5-subcase behavioral regression test that flips 4 subcases red against pre-fix source.
Fixed:

ul.li: Wide-char overwrite now zeroes the stranded mate (TerminalGrid). A wide character occupies two adjacent cells — a first half (isWideChar=true, codepoint=CP) and a continuation (isWideCont=true, codepoint=0). Before this release, three write paths left half-pairs in an inconsistent state when a new write landed on only one half: narrow writes over a continuation left the mate at col-1 still claiming isWideChar=true with no neighbor (rendered with a gap); narrow writes over a first half left the old continuation at col+1 claiming isWideCont=true with no mate (blocked selection / copy); a wide write shifted by one from an existing wide pair orphaned the old continuation at col+2; and the SIMD-coalesced fast path handleAsciiPrintRun had the same left/right edge issues on its write span. Consolidated into a single breakWidePairsAround(row, startCol, endCol) helper that runs before every write site.

description.p: xterm-compatible Background Color Erase (BCE) across every scroll and erase path. 0.7.12 Tier 2 hardening item. Locked by a 10-subcase behavioral regression test that flips 5 subcases red against pre-fix source.
Fixed:

ul.li: BCE on scroll and insert/delete paths. Apps that paint with a non-default SGR background (\e[44m etc.) and then scroll, insert lines, delete lines, delete chars, or insert blanks expect the newly-exposed cells to inherit the current background — the xterm convention vim, less, tmux, mc, and htop rely on for full-screen painted backgrounds. Pre-fix, takeBlankedCellsRow() hardcoded the default bg, so CSI L / CSI M / CSI S / CSI T and the LF-past-scroll-bottom auto-scroll all produced default-bg gaps through painted regions. deleteChars and insertBlanks used raw current-attrs bg without the validity fallback that clearRow already had. Consolidated the policy into one TerminalGrid::eraseBg() helper; every erase/scroll callsite now reads from it.

description.p: User ask 2026-04-24 — "How do I see what version of Ants Terminal I am running? Can you please add a GUI version?"
Added:

ul.li: Help → About Ants Terminal… — new rightmost menu carrying a rich-text About dialog that shows the running ANTS_VERSION, the Qt runtime version, the Lua engine version (when compiled with ANTS_LUA_PLUGINS), and a clickable GitHub homepage link. Plus a second action About Qt… routing to Qt's stock aboutQt dialog. Version is read directly from the CMake-defined ANTS_VERSION macro — no hardcoded literal that could drift on bumps.

description.p: Lua sandbox hardening trio from the 0.7.12 /indie-review. Three small defense-in-depth fixes in LuaEngine, all locked by a behavioral + source-grep regression test that flips 5 of 9 invariants red against pre-fix source.
Security:

ul.li: string.dump removed from the plugin sandbox. Lua 5.4 has no bytecode verifier — string.dump(f) returns bytecode that a crafted loader call could execute, corrupting state and escaping the sandbox. load/loadstring/loadfile are already nilled at init, but any future C API wrapping luaL_loadbuffer with plugin-supplied data would reopen the surface. Closed at the sandbox layer via lua_setfield on the already-loaded string table.

LuaEngine::loadScript forces "t" (text-only) load mode. Pre-fix, luaL_dofile forwarded to luaL_loadfilex with mode=nullptr, accepting both text and binary chunks. The 0x1b-first-byte peek was the first rejection gate; the loader call is now the second gate, so a future refactor that drops the peek still gets a rejection.

Instruction-count hook cleared before lua_close in shutdown. lua_close runs pending __gc metamethods which can execute arbitrary Lua code. If the hook fires mid-close and walks back into registry data via __ants_engine, that's a UAF window. lua_sethook(m_state, nullptr, 0, 0) before the close removes that window; C-side cleanup proceeds without any Lua-level observer.

description.p: Tier 2 hardening sweep — three open items from the 0.7.12 /indie-review landed as one release. Each fix has a behavioral regression test, source-grep on the load-bearing tokens, and was verified to fail against pre-fix source via git stash before locking. All 71 fast tests green.
Security:

ul.li: debug.log lands 0600 regardless of umask. The debug log was being created with the process umask (typically 0644 under 0022). The log can include PTY keystrokes, AI endpoint request + response bodies, OSC 133 HMAC digest material, and Claude transcript parse state. DebugLog::setActive now calls setOwnerOnlyPerms on both the opened QFileDevice and the path string immediately after open — the fd-level call covers the just-opened descriptor; the path-level call narrows any pre-existing 0644 file that append reused from a prior (pre-fix) run.

Audit path-traversal guard on finding file fields. User-supplied audit rules, audit_rules.json in a cloned project, and external scanner regex outputs can all produce findings whose file field is a traversal payload (../../etc/passwd style). Pre-fix, six call sites across AuditDialog concatenated m_projectPath + "/" + f.file and passed the result to snippet preview, comment classifier, inline-suppress scan, and the AI-triage POST body — a CWE-22 + OWASP-LLM06 exfiltration chain because the AI-triage surface forwards snippet contents to the configured chat-completions endpoint. New AuditDialog::resolveProjectPath helper canonicalizes the candidate path (resolves .. + dereferences symlinks in a single QFileInfo::canonicalFilePath call), requires the result to sit under the canonical project root with an anchored-slash prefix, and returns empty on rejection. All six call sites migrated.

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: Settings dialog discarded on external config reload. MainWindow caches the Settings dialog across Preferences… opens; the dialog was constructed with the Config pointer and populated its widgets at construction. When QFileSystemWatcher fired on an external edit, the Config pointer's target was reassigned fresh from disk but the cached dialog still held pre-reload widget state. Next Preferences… open would show stale values, and clicking OK would replay them over the fresh Config — silently undoing the external edit. onConfigFileChanged now closes the dialog if visible, schedules it via deleteLater(), and nulls the pointer so the next open rebuilds from the freshly reloaded Config.

description.p: CI un-break + tab-rename persistence. CI had been red since 0.7.17 on the AppStream metainfo validation step because the 0.7.17 release description embedded a plaintext https:// URL as an example of what the new secret-redactor scrubs, which the validator correctly rejects. Rewrote the example to use a non-URL inline form. Separately, user asked whether manual tab renames survive Ants restart — they didn't, because the pin map lived only in MainWindow memory. Fixed by bumping the session-file schema to V3 with a trailing pinnedTitle field.
Added:

ul.li: Manual tab renames persist across Ants Terminal sessions. The right-click "Rename Tab…" pin (m_tabTitlePins) is now written to and read from each per-tab session file. A tab renamed to "Deploy" or "Prod DB" or "Claude #3" keeps that label after the app exits and relaunches. SessionManager schema bumped to V3: a trailing QString pinnedTitle field appended after the V2 cwd. V2 files continue to load with the pin out-param defaulting to empty.

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: AppStream metainfo validation passes again. The 0.7.17 and 0.7.18 release descriptions failed appstreamcli validate --explain with description-has-plaintext-url. Example rewritten to non-URL form; CI build-test step now green.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: SessionManager::restore initializes optional out-params before reading. Callers that pre-populated QString *cwd or QString *pinnedTitle would previously see their sentinel survive an older-format load. Fixed by clearing both at function entry; the version-gated read blocks still only populate them when the on-disk format actually has the field.

description.p: Post-0.7.17 documentation + tech-debt sweep. Three parallel audit agents (doc drift, source debt, spec-vs-code drift) inventoried every doc-vs-code mismatch, stale TODO, dead config key, and spec line-number drift across the tree. Two real bugs landed: the Settings → General Shell picker now actually launches the user's chosen shell (previously persisted to config.json but silently dropped by the PTY launch path), and Qt::WA_OpaquePaintEvent on the menubar closes the remaining mouse-move-over-menubar dropdown flicker on KWin. The background_alpha config key was removed as redundant — user confirmed the opacity knob already does what they want (translucent terminal area, solid chrome).
Fixed:

ul.li: Config::shellCommand() actually launches the user's shell. Settings → General's Shell picker (Default / bash / zsh / fish / Custom…) has persisted the user's choice to shell_command in config.json since the original implementation, and the Settings dialog loads/re-displays it correctly. But TerminalWidget::startShell took only a working directory and invoked VtStream::start with a hardcoded empty shell arg — Pty::start's empty-shell fallback ($SHELL → pw_shell → /bin/bash) always fired, silently dropping the user's choice. Fix: startShell gains a shell parameter, the QMetaObject::invokeMethod call forwards it instead of QString(), and every production call site in MainWindow passes m_config.shellCommand().

Dropdown flicker on mouse-move over open menubar item (KWin). The menubar's autoFillBackground + stylesheet fully cover every pixel on every paint, so marking it opaque-on-paint stops Qt from invalidating the translucent parent's compositor region under it. MainWindow ctor now sets Qt::WA_OpaquePaintEvent on m_menuBar — the QSS comment always promised this was set at the construction site; the call itself was missing. Flagged in 0.7.12 Tier 3.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: README.md config-defaults table corrected. session_persistence default was shown as false but the code default in config.cpp is true. font_size range was shown as 8–32 but the code range is 4–48. Both corrected.

PLUGINS.md Lua resource-limit docs. Previously claimed limits are enforced "per plugin per event invocation" with each event getting a fresh 10 M-instruction budget. The implementation sets lua_sethook(LUA_MASKCOUNT, 10000000) once at engine init; the counter accumulates across all handlers in the same VM until the hook fires. Doc rewritten to describe the real contract.

tests/features/terminal_partial_update_mode/spec.md rewritten to match the live test. The .cpp test was updated in 0.7.4 to enforce the post-refactor invariant (TerminalWidget is a plain QWidget, no QOpenGLWidget:: calls), but the adjacent spec.md still documented the pre-0.7.4 setUpdateBehavior(PartialUpdate) fix that didn't work. Spec now matches the test.
Spec line-number drift corrected in four spec.md files: osc133_hmac_verification, session_persistence_default, review_changes_click, ai_context_redaction.

description.p: Removed:

ul.li: background_alpha config key + Config::backgroundAlpha() / setBackgroundAlpha() + TerminalWidget::setBackgroundAlpha / backgroundAlpha + m_backgroundAlpha member. User confirmed (2026-04-24) they use opacity ~0.9-0.95 to make the terminal area translucent while chrome stays solid — exactly what the opacity config key already does (paint site applies per-pixel alpha via m_windowOpacity). Since background_alpha had no UI widget, no paint-site consumer, and overlapped intent with opacity, removed entirely. Stale key in existing config.json files is harmlessly ignored on load (Qt's JSON parser tolerates unknown keys).

description.p: Hot-path parser optimization plus two /indie-review security follow-ups. VtParser printable-ASCII runs now coalesce into a single VtAction carrying a pointer+length slice of the feed buffer, and TerminalGrid::handleAsciiPrintRun batches per-row cell writes so markScreenDirty, the combining-char erase, and the cell() clamp all fire once per row instead of once per byte. Rebench on 8 MiB corpora: ascii_print 24.4 → 30.7 MB/s (+26%), ansi_sgr 27.5 → 31.4 MB/s (+14%). RIS (ESC c) now preserves all 8 integration callbacks — previously tput reset silenced desktop notifications, progress bars, command-finished plugin events, user-var theming hooks, and the OSC 133 HMAC forgery alarm permanently. Two new token-saving developer hooks for Claude Code users land alongside the perf work: a UserPromptSubmit git-context hook that feeds <git-context> to Claude on every prompt (saving ~400–600 tokens per turn that would otherwise go to a Bash(git status) call), and OWASP LLM06 secret redaction on the outbound AI-dialog request body.
Added:

ul.li: Claude Code UserPromptSubmit git-context hook. New Settings button "Install git-context hook" installs ~/.config/ants-terminal/hooks/claude-git-context.sh and merges a hooks.UserPromptSubmit entry into ~/.claude/settings.json. The script emits a compact <git-context> block (branch, upstream, ahead/behind counts, staged / unstaged / untracked file counts) on every user prompt so Claude Code sees repo state without spending tokens on a Bash(git status) round-trip. Global scope — installed at user level so every project inherits. No-op outside git repos / when git isn't on PATH.

src/secretredact.h — OWASP LLM06 defense layer. Header-only module exposing SecretRedact::scrub(QString). Regex-scrubs 14 well-known secret shapes: AWS AKIA/ASIA, GitHub classic / OAuth / app / fine-grained PATs, Anthropic, OpenAI project + legacy, Slack, Stripe, JWT, Bearer <token> headers, generic api_key=/token=/password=/secret= assignments, and multi-line PEM private-key blocks. Replacements are [REDACTED:<kind>] so the LLM still sees the surrounding command shape. Priority-ordered so sk-ant- is labelled anthropic, not mislabelled as legacy openai.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: VtAction carries an optional coalesced Print run. Two new fields point into the caller's feed buffer (valid for the duration of the ActionCallback call only). VtStream::onPtyData's batch path materializes runs into per-byte Print actions before queuing for the GUI thread, since its batch outlives the feed buffer. Lifetime invariant documented in vtparser.h.

AiDialog::sendRequest scrubs both outbound strings. The terminal scrollback context and the user's own question both pass through SecretRedact::scrub before reaching the JSON body. When redactedCount > 0, the dialog appends a System chat-history note so the user knows the payload differs from what they saw. Chat history's "You:" display intentionally shows pre-redaction text (redaction is a network-boundary concern, not a UX one).

description.p: Security:

ul.li: Claude allowlist settings.local.json perms survive rename fallback. ClaudeAllowlistDialog::saveSettings previously set 0600 only on the QSaveFile temp fd. Most local filesystems preserve fd perms across rename(2), but FAT/exFAT on removable media don't carry POSIX perm bits, some SMB/NFS servers apply umask to the rename destination, and Qt's QSaveFile::commit falls back to copy+unlink on cross-device renames — in all three cases the final file landed at the process umask (typically 0644). Since the file can hold Claude Code bearer tokens, the widened perms were a credential-leak surface. Fix: after commit() returns true, re-apply setOwnerOnlyPerms on the final path (the pre-commit fd-level chmod is retained — neither call subsumes the other). Flagged by the 2026-04-23 /indie-review.

AI context + user-message secret redaction (OWASP LLM06). The AI dialog previously shipped recent scrollback verbatim to whichever endpoint the user had configured — a single cat .env, aws configure show, or git clone with an embedded ghp_ token in the URL in scrollback exfiltrated credentials. Complements the 0.7.12 LLM01/LLM02 fix (ai_insert_command_sanitize) — that handled AI → PTY; this handles PTY → AI.

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: Right-click "Rename Tab…" pins the label. User report 2026-04-24: Claude Code's per-prompt OSC 0/2 title writes wiped manual tab names within seconds. The rename handler wrote directly to setTabText without populating m_tabTitlePins, so the per-shell titleChanged handler and the 2 s tick both saw no pin and relabelled. Rename now routes through setTabTitleForRemote so non-empty names pin and empty names clear the pin — giving the user an in-UI "un-rename" path that didn't exist before.

RIS (ESC c) preserves all 8 integration callbacks + m_osc133Key. Previously notifyCallback, progressCallback, lineCompletionCallback, commandFinishedCallback, userVarCallback, and osc133ForgeryCallback silently became empty functions after any tput reset. Security impact for the forgery alarm: a well-timed reset silenced it permanently, enabling OSC 133 forgery to proceed unseen. m_osc133Key (read from $ANTS_OSC133_KEY at process start) also survives RIS.

description.p: Performance:

ul.li: VtParser printable-ASCII run coalescing. SIMD fast path (scanSafeAsciiRun on SSE2/NEON) emits one VtAction::Print per run instead of one per byte. For a 10 KB TUI-repaint PTY read, allocation count drops from ~10 000 to 1 and dispatch count drops by the same factor. 8 MiB ascii_print 327 → 260 ms (−20%), ansi_sgr 291 → 255 ms (−12%). newline_stream and utf8_cjk are bound by scroll and UTF-8 decoding respectively, so their numbers are flat — no coalesce-applicable runs.

TerminalGrid::handleAsciiPrintRun batches per-row writes. Skips the per-byte wcwidth, combining-char branch, wide-char branch, and std::clamp inside cell(). Fires markScreenDirty once per row instead of once per byte.

description.p: Per-tab Claude Code activity indicator — a small state-dependent dot at the leading edge of each tab whose shell has a Claude Code child process, so users running Claude across several tabs can see at a glance which one is waiting on them without cycling focus. Permission prompts route by the hook event's session_id (matched against the <session-uuid>.jsonl transcript filename) rather than the active-tab heuristic, so a prompt emitted in tab 3 lights up tab 3 specifically.
Added:

ul.li: Per-tab Claude activity dot. Colour encodes state: muted grey for Idle / Thinking, blue for generic ToolUse, green for Bash (split out as the highest-signal tool), cyan for Planning, violet for Compacting, and a bright orange dot with a white outline for AwaitingInput. Hover tooltip on each tab spells out the state ("Claude: thinking…", "Claude: Bash", etc.) so colours can be disambiguated without switching tabs.

Settings → General: "Show per-tab Claude activity dot" checkbox. Flips claude_tab_status_indicator (default on); change takes effect on the next paint via the live config reload path, no restart needed.

New src/claudetabtracker.{h,cpp}. Keys state by shell PID so tab reorder / close doesn't shift entries, and reuses the existing transcript parser through a shared static helper — no duplicated parsing logic. Contract and 11-invariant regression test at tests/features/claude_tab_status_indicator/.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: ClaudeIntegration::parseTranscriptForState refactored to a thin wrapper around a new static parseTranscriptTail helper. Pure function, no side effects — safe to call from per-shell trackers. Behavior unchanged; claude_status_bar and claude_plan_mode_detection regression tests still green against the same transcripts.

Hook events stash session_id in ClaudeIntegration::lastHookSessionId(). Handlers of signals emitted by processHookEvent (e.g. permissionRequested) can now read the id to route the event per-session rather than treating every hook as "belongs to the active tab" — required for the per-tab indicator to flag the correct tab on PermissionRequest.

description.p: Fold-in of the 2026-04-23 re-review follow-up list. Six of nine items had already shipped in 0.7.12 but were never crossed off the ROADMAP; the remaining three are real new work — a /tmp/kwin_*.js orphan sweep, a behavioral replacement for a fragile source-grep gate test, and a signal-count assertion on the plan-mode tail-preservation path. Two new regression tests lock existing invariants.
Added:

ul.li: Startup sweep for orphan /tmp/kwin_*_ants_*.js script files. KWin helper scripts are written via QTemporaryFile(autoRemove=false) and removed by a chained dbus callback after KWin unloads them; a crash, SIGKILL, or dbus hang between write and unload orphans the file. MainWindow ctor now runs a once-per-process sweep of kwin_{pos,move,center}_ants_*.js older than one hour, avoiding a race with any in-flight script another Ants instance just wrote.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: Remote-control opt-in gate test is now behavioral, not source-grep. The 0.7.12 test grepped for remoteControlEnabled() within 400 chars of m_remoteControl->start() — a getter rename or helper-hoist would have silently defeated the gate while still passing. Replaced with a Config API round-trip (default false, setter persists across instances, value readable post-reload); a getter rename now fails at compile-time. The structural "start() must live inside a conditional" check is retained as a plain-substring lookback to catch outright gate removal — without the catastrophic-backtracking risk that killed the initial regex attempt.

Plan-mode regression test asserts emission count, not just final value. runToggleFreeTailPreservesState now confirms planModeChanged fires exactly once across both parse phases (seed toggles NotSet → plan = one emission; tail sees zero permission-mode events and must not re-fire). Guards against a regression that re-emits on every parse.

description.p: Tests:

ul.li: config_parse_failure_guard adds retention-cap invariant (INV-5). Plants 8 stale .corrupt-* siblings with decreasing mtimes, trips a fresh parse-failure, asserts only the newest 5 (fresh + 4 old) remain. Verified to fail against a neutered prune loop (got 9, expected 5). Sets mtimes via QFile::setFileTime since QDir::Time ranks on mtime, not on the filename-embedded timestamp.

remote_control_opt_in adds behavioral Config round-trip section. Sandboxed via XDG_CONFIG_HOME (not setTestModeEnabled — that routes to a Qt-test-specific path which has collided with real user configs in practice). Four invariants: fresh-default false, in-memory round-trip, cross-instance persistence, setter-back-to-false round-trip.

description.p: Internal: ROADMAP re-review follow-up list reconciled with shipped code — config backup retention cap, remote-control gate cache, kwin TOCTOU fix header-comment accuracy, spec.md timestamp format, and plugin/theme parse warnings were all landed by 0.7.12 but still listed as outstanding. Flipped with "Shipped 0.7.12" notes; no code changes for these items, just book-keeping reconciliation.

Three Claude Code integration robustness fixes from the 0.7.12+1 re-review checkpoint. All three failed silently — no errors, no degraded-mode logs — so the symptoms were a transcript-driven UI state that simply stopped updating, or a project that appeared under the wrong name in the picker. All three now have targeted fixture-based regression tests.
Fixed:

ul.li: Transcript tail window grows past large trailing events. parseTranscriptForState previously read the final 32 KB of the JSONL transcript and skipped the first line after the seek as likely-truncated; when the final event was a user tool_result carrying inline file contents, the window landed inside that one event and the parser returned zero events, freezing UI state. Replaced with a doubling-grow window (32 KB → 4 MiB cap) that only trims a potentially-partial prefix line when the buffer contains at least two newlines.

Transcript dialog renders thinking content blocks. ClaudeTranscriptDialog::formatEntry only handled text, tool_use, and tool_result; thinking fell into an implicit else and was discarded. Now rendered as italicized, dimmed Thinking: paragraphs, distinguishing chain-of-thought from the visible reply.

decodeProjectPath preserves hyphens in project names. Claude Code encodes absolute paths by replacing every / with - — lossy, since my-project (leaf) and my/project (two segments) produce the same string. The old decoder replaced every - with / unconditionally. Rewrote as a greedy left-to-right filesystem-probing walker: at each hyphen, prefer the / form if that directory exists, fall back to - otherwise. extractCwdFromTranscript remains the preferred source of truth; the probe-based decoder is the last-resort fallback.

description.p: Tests: new claude_transcript_robustness feature lane with six invariants covering tail-window growth past a 40 KB trailing event, the 4 MiB growth cap, leaf- and intermediate-segment hyphen preservation, legacy all-slashes fallback when filesystem hints are absent, and canonical repo-style decoding. Verified to fail against pre-fix code on the regression invariants.

Scope-tightening pass on the audit rule-pack trust boundary shipped in 0.7.12. The global audit_trust_command_rules bool over-granted after one opt-in — trusting Ants's own repo implicitly trusted every cloned repo the user later opened. 0.7.13 replaces the global flag with a per-project hash-bound trust store so trust is both narrow and self-invalidating.
Security:

ul.li: Per-project audit rule-pack trust store. Global audit_trust_command_rules bool is retired. Trust is keyed on (canonical projectPath, sha256(audit_rules.json bytes)) via three new Config methods: isAuditRulePackTrusted, trustAuditRulePack, untrustAuditRulePack. Trusting one project no longer extends to siblings; any edit to the rule pack invalidates trust (re-prompt on next open — silent post-trust modification is detected); projectPath is canonicalized via QFileInfo::canonicalFilePath so symlinks and trailing slashes resolve to a single record; ANTS_AUDIT_TRUST_UNSAFE=1 remains an escape hatch for CI. Users with the legacy bool set to true are re-prompted per project on upgrade.

description.p: Changed:

ul.li: Audit dialog: "Untrusted rules: N" badge. The skipped-rule count now appears on the Detected-types row alongside "User rules: N" and "Path rules: N", with a tooltip explaining the trust boundary and opt-in path. Stderr qWarning copy retained for headless / CI cases.

CLAUDE.md streamlined. Compressed from 325 to 147 lines (~67% reduction) by dropping the full project-structure tree, collapsing the verbose audit pipeline prose, retiring the per-dep install-command list, and dropping the Config-keys table. Retained: non-obvious gotchas, rationale for design decisions, and the release-file triad rule.

description.p: Tests: new tests/features/audit_command_rule_trust/ lane — seven-invariant regression test against the new trust API (default-untrusted, round-trip, hash-bound invalidation, cross-project isolation with byte-identical packs, path canonicalization through symlinks + trailing slashes, idempotent untrust, cross-Config-instance persistence through config.json). Spec-first: contract written before the code.

Independent-review sweep. A multi-agent /indie-review run across 14 subsystems surfaced 60 HIGH findings; the ship-this-week tier landed in this tag, followed by a re-review of the shipped batch that caught three more HIGH findings of the same shape, plus a polish pass handling the six mechanical follow-ups. Every fix carries a regression test anchored to an external signal — published spec, CVE, real on-disk data — not to the author's own reasoning. Closes the self-graded-homework loop.
Security:

ul.li: Remote-control opt-in by default. New remote_control_enabled config key (default false, matches Kitty's allow_remote_control=no). send-text payloads pass through a C0 control-char filter stripping {0x00..0x08, 0x0B..0x1F, 0x7F} while preserving HT/LF/CR and UTF-8 multi-byte, unless the request carries "raw": true. Closes the UID-scope keystroke-injection chain. The gate snapshots once per process (static bool) so a second window reading the config after a runtime toggle matches the "requires restart" documentation.

Silent-data-loss guard at three sites (src/config.cpp, src/claudeallowlist.cpp, src/settingsdialog.cpp — Install Claude hooks). All three now rotate the corrupt file aside via the shared rotateCorruptFileAside() helper (ms-precision timestamp + collision-retry up to 10 suffixes, keep-newest-5 retention cap), log the backup path, and refuse to write further until the user intervenes. Settings dialog shows the backup path in a QMessageBox.
std::rename return checked in Config::save. Failure logs errno and removes the orphan tmp file instead of silently stranding it on disk.

SSH bookmark extraArgs sanitizer. Rejects -oProxyCommand=, -oLocalCommand=, -oPermitLocalCommand=yes, -oMatch=exec, and -oKnownHostsCommand= in both single-token and space-separated forms, case-insensitive. Closes the CVE-2017-1000117-adjacent RCE-via-bookmark-field surface.

AI Insert Cmd sanitize + confirm. New AiDialog::extractAndSanitizeCommand strips C0 controls (minus HT/LF/CR), DEL, C1 controls (incl. NEL U+0085), line/paragraph separators, bidi overrides (Trojan Source, CVE-2021-42574), and zero-width codepoints. Capped at 4 KiB. Click handler requires explicit QMessageBox::question confirmation showing the literal bytes + "N bytes filtered" footer; truncation warning fires when the 500-char preview doesn't cover the full command. OWASP LLM01 + LLM02 mitigation. Language-hint heuristic now requires the first line be a single unbroken token before stripping it.

Audit rule-pack command fields gated on Config::auditTrustCommandRules() (default false) or ANTS_AUDIT_TRUST_UNSAFE=1. A cloned-but-untrusted repo can no longer run arbitrary shell via audit_rules.json.
/tmp/kwin_*.js migrated to QTemporaryFile at three callsites. Closes the same-name symlink-swap TOCTOU and the two-instance collision.

description.p: Fixed:

ul.li: Claude plan-mode detection. Was reading value("mode") — a field that never appears in Claude Code v2.1.87 JSONL. Fixed to permissionMode; toggle-free tail scan now preserves the latched state.
Plugin manifest + user theme parse warnings. Both loaders now emit qWarning with the parse-error byte offset so users can find their own typos.

description.p: Tests: five new feature-test lanes in tests/features/ (claude_plan_mode_detection, remote_control_opt_in, config_parse_failure_guard, ssh_extra_args_sanitize, ai_insert_command_sanitize), each anchored to a published spec, CVE, or live on-disk bytes.

Housekeeping pass — two long-deferred items from the 0.8+ queue land early, plus a ROADMAP correction. The perf item rewrites the combining-char map shift in deleteChars / insertBlanks to use node-handle extract() + key mutate + insert() instead of rebuilding the map. The security item is a defence-in-depth stat-guard around the remote-control socket's /tmp fallback cleanup.
Changes:

ul.li: Changed — combining-char map shift without rebuild (src/terminalgrid.cpp, deleteChars / insertBlanks). Old impl built a fresh std::unordered_map, moved each surviving entry into it (re-hashing every key), then move-assigned it back. Rewrote to collect affected keys in two small local vectors, erase the deleted ones, then for each shift extract the node handle, mutate its integer key, and reinsert — zero bucket reallocation, zero re-hashing of the value vectors. deleteChars processes shifts ascending; insertBlanks descending. Empty-map fast-path skips the block entirely, so steady-state rows pay nothing. Tests combining_on_resize and osc8_insert_delete_lines still pass.

Security — IPC-socket /tmp fallback stat-guard (src/remotecontrol.cpp). RemoteControl::start() used to call QLocalServer::removeServer(path) unconditionally on stale-socket recovery. The XDG_RUNTIME_DIR path is 0700-owned and safe, but the /tmp/ants-terminal-<uid>.sock fallback lives in shared-world-writable space where a symlink or foreign file could trick us into unlinking something unrelated. Added safeToUnlinkLocalSocket() that lstat()s the path and refuses removal unless it's an actual S_ISSOCK owned by getuid(). ENOENT passes through. On refusal the remote-control layer disables itself with a clear log message — no silent unlink of unknown files.

Fixed — ROADMAP accuracy. The 💭 entry for "Per-frame QString construction in the text-run accumulator" was actually shipped in 0.7.9 (both TerminalWidget::paintEvent and GlRenderer::render accumulate codepoints into a reusable std::vector<char32_t> on the Run struct and call QString::fromUcs4(data, size) once at run-push time). Promoted to ✅ with a retrospective write-up matching the free-list entry.

description.p: Follow-through on the 0.7.9 null-result. The std::rotate scroll refactor was rejected because it paid for save/restore overhead without touching the real bottleneck — the per-scroll vector<Cell>(m_cols) heap allocation for the new bottom row. This release addresses that bottleneck directly with a small free-list of m_cols-sized cell buffers fed by any path that discards a row (scrollback eviction, scrollDown, insertLines, deleteLines) and drained by paths that need a fresh blank row. Steady-state scroll work on a full scrollback runs allocator-free.

ul.li: Changed — TerminalGrid cell-buffer free-list. New m_freeCellBuffers pool (cap 4 entries) + takeBlankedCellsRow() / returnCellsRow() helpers. scrollUp, scrollDown, insertLines, deleteLines now recycle cell buffers instead of calling makeRow(m_cols, …) on every scroll. When scrollback hits m_maxScrollback and a pop_front evicts the oldest line, its cells vector is salvaged into the pool; the next scroll's new bottom row pulls from it (blanked in place), skipping the allocator. resize() clears the pool. Pool stays at 0–2 entries in normal use. All 49 feature tests pass; scroll_region_rotate (shipped 0.7.9 to lock the contract) verifies the invariants held across the refactor.

Performance — newline_stream 5.26 → 6.09 MB/s (+15.8 %). Median of ten-run pairs. Other corpora are flat to mildly improved: ascii_print ~23 MB/s, ansi_sgr ~16.5 MB/s, utf8_cjk 8.9 MB/s (+12 % — CJK payload also hits scrollUp). First perf result to validate the 0.7.9 thesis that the container shuffle was a red herring and the allocator was the actual hot spot.

description.p: Planned-work pass — three ROADMAP items from the 0.7.7 "deferred to 0.8+" queue investigated and shipped or rejected with findings on record. The investigations that didn't ship still left value behind (a feature-test that locks scroll-region semantics for any future refactor), and the one that did ship consolidates 13 path strings through one header and halves the per-frame QString::fromUcs4 call count in both render paths.

ul.li: Added — scroll_region_rotate feature-test. Locks the observable behavior of TerminalGrid::scrollUp / scrollDown: row motion inside [scrollTop, scrollBottom], outside-region invariance, main-screen scrollback push, alt-screen no push, hyperlink tracking, and count > regionHeight clamp. Six scenarios, eight invariants. Pins the contract so the 0.8 ring-buffer-screen work can refactor safely.

Added — src/configpaths.h. Inline namespace consolidating ~/.claude/projects, ~/.claude/sessions, ~/.claude/settings.json, ~/.claude/projects/<enc>/memory/MEMORY.md, and ~/.config/ants-terminal/hooks/claude-forward.sh. 13 duplicated path-concat sites across claudeintegration.cpp / settingsdialog.cpp collapsed to one call each. Future XDG-respect pass now has a single update surface.

Changed — render path per-run QString::fromUcs4. Both the QPainter (terminalwidget.cpp) and GL (glrenderer.cpp) paths now accumulate codepoints into a std::vector<char32_t> per run and build the QString once at flush/draw time, replacing the previous runText += QString::fromUcs4(&cp, 1) pattern that allocated a small QString per non-space cell and reallocated the run string on every append. Ligature shaping via QTextLayout still receives the run text at the same point; behaviour identical.

Investigated, not shipped — std::rotate scroll refactor. Measured 12–15% regression on bench_vt_throughput newline_stream (5.2 → 4.6 MB/s) because the common count == 1 case has rotate paying for a save/restore cycle that vector::erase + push_back avoids. Real bottleneck is the per-scroll row allocation, not container shuffle. Reverted; test stays. ROADMAP updated with scrollback-push cell-reuse as the real 0.8 candidate.

Investigated, not shipped — DialogBase / ManagedProcess. ROADMAP over-sold both: only 2 dialogs use the Ok/Apply/Cancel pattern (not 6), and auditdialog.cpp uses one shared QProcess member (not 6 copies). Both deferred until more justification.

description.p: Audit fold-in — every regression guard the 0.7.6 / 0.7.7 hardening pass left behind is now a repo-wide detector. Three new rules in the Project Audit dialog turn the one-off sweeps into persistent guards, so the next contributor who re-introduces the pattern gets caught at audit-time.

ul.li: Added — ssh_argv_dash_host audit rule (Security, Major). Flags any << shellQuote(…host…) argv construction without a preceding args << "--" argv terminator — the CVE-2017-1000117 class. Runtime filter drops findings when the guard appears within ±5 lines (spans the if (!user.isEmpty()) / else split in sshdialog.cpp:67-71).

Added — qimage_load_without_peek audit rule (Qt, Minor). Flags .loadFromData( calls that aren't tagged // image-peek-ok or preceded by a QImageReader size-peek within ±5 lines — the image-bomb DoS vector that the 0.7.6 hardening sweep fixed. Both shipped sites in terminalgrid.cpp (OSC 1337 PNG + Kitty graphics) carry the reviewer sign-off tag and drop cleanly.

Added — setPermissions_pair_no_helper audit rule (Qt, Info). Hygiene nudge toward setOwnerOnlyPerms() from src/secureio.h when a raw setPermissions(QFileDevice::ReadOwner | QFileDevice::WriteOwner) bitmask appears. Pattern excludes the 0755 hook-script case (extra | flags after WriteOwner) so settingsdialog.cpp's one literal bitmask doesn't false-fire.
Changed — audit self-test coverage: three new run_rule lines in tests/audit_self_test.sh, coverage cross-check sees the new rule ids automatically. 55/55 pass (up from 52/52).

description.p: The 0.7.7 planned-work pass from the hardening four-dimensional review — every item marked as planned in ROADMAP.md's 0.7.7 section is now shipped. Two de-duplication refactors, three paint-path perf hoists, four regression feature-tests locking the 0.7.6 hardening fixes.

ul.li: Added — regression coverage for the four 0.7.7 hardening fixes: tests/features/osc8_insert_delete_lines/ (CSI L / CSI M hyperlink sync invariant), tests/features/hyperlink_resize_clamp/ (shrink-resize clamp on the active OSC 8 start coordinates), tests/features/ssh_dash_host_rejected/ (the -- argv terminator against CVE-2017-1000117 class hosts, 5 scenarios), and tests/features/image_bomb_png_header_peek/ (static source-inspection guard ensuring every loadFromData is peeked first). Each test is designed to fail on pre-fix code and pass now. 49 feature/fast tests total.

Changed — setOwnerOnlyPerms helper (src/secureio.h) replaces the 11 copies of file.setPermissions(QFileDevice::ReadOwner | QFileDevice::WriteOwner) scattered across config.cpp, sessionmanager.cpp (×2), claudeallowlist.cpp, auditdialog.cpp (×4), claudeintegration.cpp (×2), remotecontrol.cpp, and auditrulequality.cpp. One typo (ReadOther, ReadGroup) away from silently widening access to files that may hold an API key. Two overloads: one for open QFileDevice& handles, one for existing path strings. The one 0755 case in settingsdialog.cpp (hooks shell script — needs executable bit) stays literal by design.

Changed — shellQuote helper (src/shellutils.h) replaces the previous static function in sshdialog.cpp and the re-defined lambda in mainwindow.cpp::openClaudeProjectsDialog. The two earlier implementations differed on empty-string and no-special-chars handling; both call sites now share one inline implementation.

Changed — paint-path hoists: QFontMetrics is no longer constructed per underlined cell; updateFontMetrics() caches underlinePos and lineWidth into members refreshed on font change. Selection bounds are now normalised once per paintEvent into local scalars with an inline cellInSelection lambda, rather than running std::swap + std::min/std::max per cell. Measurable on search-highlighted + selection-active frames.

Changed — isCellSearchMatch now uses std::lower_bound instead of linear scan. m_searchMatches is sorted by globalLine; the binary search jumps to the first match at or after the target line and iterates only the matches on that line. The previous implementation self-described as "binary search" but walked the whole vector from index 0 on every cell. Expected win: 5–20% of paint time on search-highlighted content in deep scrollback.

description.p: Closes the 0.7.x remote-control arc and the audit-signal gap opened by the 0.7.5 hygiene work. Seven rc_protocol commands now cover the "drive the terminal from a script" story end-to-end (ls, send-text, new-tab, select-window, set-title, get-text, launch), and the Project Audit panel gains two feature-coverage lanes that catch shipped features without locking tests and spec prose that drifted past a rename.

ul.li: Added — Audit feature-coverage lanes: two in-process checks in src/featurecoverage.{cpp,h}. Lane 1 (spec↔code drift) extracts backtick-fenced identifier tokens from tests/features/*/spec.md and reports ones that no longer appear under src/. Lane 2 (CHANGELOG↔feature-test coverage) fuzz-matches Added/Fixed bullets in the top CHANGELOG section against feature-spec titles. Both lanes plug into the existing parseFindings → suppress → render pipeline via AuditCheck::inProcessRunner. Pinned by 17 invariants in tests/features/feature_coverage/.

Added — remote-control launch: seventh and final rc_protocol command. Spawns a command in a new tab in one round-trip, auto-appending a trailing newline — the convenience differentiator from new-tab, which stays byte-faithful.

Added — remote-control get-text: returns the trailing N lines of (scrollback + screen), default 100, capped server-side at 10 000. Reuses the AI dialog's context-capture accessor so format stays consistent.
Added — remote-control set-title: pins a tab label against both OSC 0/2 shell updates and the 2 s refresh timer. Empty title clears the pin; pin storage cleaned up on tab close.
Added — remote-control select-window: switches the active tab by 0-based index; focus follows so chained send-text lands on the new pane.

Added — remote-control new-tab: opens a fresh tab and returns its index; optional cwd and command. 200 ms settle before writing the initial command; QPointer-guarded against tab-close-before-fire races.

Added — remote-control send-text: writes UTF-8 bytes byte-for-byte to a tab's PTY. Control chars and escapes pass through unchanged, matching Kitty's rc_protocol send-text semantics. Reads stdin until EOF when --remote-text is absent.

Added — remote-control protocol scaffold: Kitty-style JSON-over-Unix-socket RPC. QLocalServer on $ANTS_REMOTE_SOCKET or $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/ants-terminal.sock; LF-terminated one-request/one-response. The same binary doubles as the client via --remote <cmd>.

Added — main-thread stall detector: 200 ms heartbeat QTimer in MainWindow measures gap between its own firings; logs drift + cumulative count + worst-case when the event loop was blocked. Gated by ANTS_DEBUG=perf, zero cost when off.

Added — VT throughput benchmark harness: drives four fixed corpora (ascii_print, newline_stream, ansi_sgr, utf8_cjk) through VtParser → TerminalGrid at -O2, emits CSV. Registered under ctest label perf; first step of the 0.8.0 throughput investigation.

Changed — dropdown-flicker suppression extended to QEvent::HoverMove / HoverEnter / HoverLeave in MainWindow::eventFilter. The 0.7.5 NoAnimStyle fix only partially reduced the mouse-movement-triggered flicker; Qt's style engine consults WA_Hover independently of QMouseEvent. Residual flicker still visible — pushed to the 0.8.0 roadmap for a different-angle fix.

Fixed — new-tab caller-owns-newline contract: first ship used writeCommand (auto-appends \n), contradicting the documented send-text-style semantics. newTabForRemote now uses sendToPty (raw bytes). Convenience "open + run" moved to the new launch command.

description.p: Same-day follow-up to 0.7.4 that resolves the dropdown-menu flicker the 0.7.4 "Known Issue" section called out. Root cause finally pinpointed via the Debug Mode instrumentation that shipped in 0.7.4: Fusion's QWidgetAnimator was creating a 60 Hz QPropertyAnimation(target=QWidget, prop=geometry) cycle on the idle window — 1439 animation completions in a 23 s idle log, each driving a full LayoutRequest → UpdateRequest → Paint cascade. Neither setEffectEnabled(UI_AnimateMenu, false) nor QMainWindow::setAnimated(false) covers the style-hint-driven cycle.

ul.li: Fixed — dropdown-menu flicker (root cause): a NoAnimStyle QProxyStyle in src/main.cpp zeroes SH_Widget_Animation_Duration and SH_Widget_Animate, wrapped around Fusion at startup. Idle-log result: 0 QPropertyAnimation events (was ≈62 Hz in 0.7.4). Total event volume dropped >99% (29 096 → 62 log lines in 4 s idle). Pinned by the new no_anim_style feature test.

Fixed — Debug Mode animation-creation hook: switched from QEvent::ChildAdded to QEvent::ChildPolished. At ChildAdded time the child's vtable still points at QObject (derived ctor hasn't run) so the QPropertyAnimation filter never matched — silently useless on the 0.7.4 investigation. ChildPolished fires post-construction and now correctly logs animation creation sites.

description.p: A session's worth of user-facing UI fixes plus a new Debug Mode that made them possible. Tools → Debug Mode gives 14 independent category toggles (Paint, Events, Input, PTY, VT, Render, Plugins, Network, Config, Audit, Claude, Signals, Shell, Session) that write timestamped lines to ~/.local/share/ants-terminal/debug.log, enabled narrowly (one category) or broadly (all). The instrument pinpointed a ~54 Hz full-window repaint cascade that had been driving the menubar / dropdown / paste-dialog flicker across several releases.

ul.li: Added — Debug Mode: env-var bootstrap (ANTS_DEBUG=all or ANTS_DEBUG=paint,events,vt) or in-app Tools → Debug Mode menu with checkable categories + Open Log File + Clear Log actions. Hot-path cost when disabled is a single bit-test.

Added — OSC 133 prompt-on-new-line guard: when a shell emits OSC 133 A (prompt start) and the cursor is not at column 0, the terminal nudges it to a new line first. Fixes "prompt glued onto the end of the previous command's output" (e.g. cat file.json with no trailing newline). Equivalent to zsh's PROMPT_SP, but terminal-side so it works for bash / fish / any shell that emits OSC 133.

Fixed — menubar hover flash: QMenuBar now sets autoFillBackground(true) + Qt::WA_StyledBackground so the stylesheet background-color paints reliably under a WA_TranslucentBackground parent; :hover mirrors :selected to close the one-frame pre-selected hover gap; setNativeMenuBar(false) made explicit.

Fixed — paste-confirmation dialog: rewrote to the fully async Review-Changes pattern (heap QDialog + WA_DeleteOnClose + show()/raise()/activateWindow(), no exec(), no QEventLoop). Three prior attempts via QMessageBox::exec, QDialog::exec, and show()+QEventLoop all hit the ApplicationModal-vs-frameless-parent click-swallow regression that was originally diagnosed for the Review-Changes dialog at 0.6.29.

Fixed — Command Palette idle timer churn: the palette's hidden QListWidget ran its QAbstractItemView timer machinery at ~50 Hz from MainWindow construction onward, cascading a full-window repaint every frame. Lazy-create the list in CommandPalette::show() so the view doesn't exist until the user first presses Ctrl+Shift+P.
Fixed — QMainWindow dock animator: setAnimated(false). Ants has no dock widgets, but QMainWindow's built-in QWidgetAnimator ran continuously at idle.

Fixed — Qt UI effect animations disabled globally (UI_AnimateMenu, UI_FadeMenu, UI_AnimateCombo, UI_AnimateTooltip, UI_FadeTooltip, UI_AnimateToolBox). These drove QPropertyAnimation(geometry) at 60 Hz on menu open/close; we have no use case for animated chrome.

Fixed — titlebar button tooltips removed. A Qt / KWin / Wayland quirk fired the QTipLabel show/hide cycle on every frame while the cursor was near a titlebar button, animating the tooltip's geometry at ~60 Hz. Diagnosed via the debug-log system. Button glyphs (✥ / ✕ / ⬜ / ⟩) + hover-colour feedback give enough affordance without tooltip text.

Changed — TerminalWidget base class: QOpenGLWidget → plain QWidget. The GL widget composites its FBO through the parent's backing store and that always invalidates the top-level; PartialUpdate, WA_OpaquePaintEvent, swapInterval(0), and alpha-buffer opt-out all failed to break it. The QPainter path is unaffected (still ligature-aware via QTextLayout / HarfBuzz). The optional GL glyph-atlas renderer is dormant in 0.7.4 and returns later as an embedded QOpenGLWindow via createWindowContainer.
Tests — menubar_hover_stylesheet, paste_dialog_custom, and new terminal_partial_update_mode pin the invariants above (35/35 pass).

description.p: H6.1 of the 📦 distribution-adoption plan lands: Lua plugins inside the Flatpak sandbox. The 0.7.2 manifest shipped with plugin support disabled because org.kde.Sdk//6.7 does not carry lua54-devel and flathub/shared-modules has no Lua 5.4 entry today. 0.7.3 closes that gap with an in-manifest Lua archive module, so a Flatpak build loads plugins the same way the openSUSE, Arch, and Debian builds do.

ul.li: Added — Lua 5.4 archive module in packaging/flatpak/org.ants.Terminal.yml: a dedicated lua module appears before ants-terminal, built from the upstream Lua 5.4.7 source tarball with a pinned sha256 and the linux-noreadline target (Ants links liblua.a statically — pulling readline into the sandbox would be pure bloat). MYCFLAGS="-fPIC" keeps the archive PIE-safe for linking into the ants-terminal executable; installed to /app via make install INSTALL_TOP=/app, which is exactly where CMake's FindLua searches by default when CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/app.

Added — auto-refresh of the Lua tarball hash: the lua module carries an x-checker-data: stanza pointing at the upstream Lua FTP listing with version-pattern: lua-(5\.4\.\d+)\.tar\.gz and a url-template that substitutes the matched version into the canonical tarball URL. Flathub CI opens a PR against the Flathub repo with a refreshed url + sha256 on each Lua 5.4.x point release — no manual hash churn, and 5.5.x majors are excluded (they would break the in-source find_package(Lua 5.4) floor).

Added — feature test tests/features/flatpak_lua_module/: source-grep regression pinning six invariants against the manifest YAML — Lua module precedes ants-terminal, type: archive with a pinned sha256:, MYCFLAGS="-fPIC" in the build commands, make install INSTALL_TOP=/app, the linux-noreadline target (guarding against the default make linux alias to linux-readline), and the x-checker-data stanza with the 5.4.x version-pattern.

Changed — packaging/flatpak/README.md: the "Lua plugins — limitation" section is replaced with a "Lua plugins" section documenting the enabled path (archive-module build, linux-noreadline rationale, x-checker-data automation, success criterion of PluginManager loading a plugin inside the sandbox). Manifest header comment updated to describe the in-manifest module and the automated hash-refresh path.
Changed — ROADMAP.md H6.1 flipped from 📋 to ✅ (shipped in 0.7.3).

description.p: H6 of the 📦 distribution-adoption plan lands: Flatpak packaging. One artifact that runs on every distro that ships flatpak, with a PTY path wired for the sandbox so the user's shell reaches the host's $PATH and filesystem. Unblocks the "packaged everywhere" roadmap gate without per-distro maintenance.

ul.li: Added — Flatpak manifest (packaging/flatpak/org.ants.Terminal.yml): targets org.kde.Platform//6.7 so the KDE SDK brings cmake, ninja, and every Qt6 component the build needs. finish-args cover Wayland + fallback-X11 + DRI, network (AI endpoint + SSH), portals (org.freedesktop.portal.* for global shortcuts / file dialogs), desktop notifications, and XDG config/data directories. Single cmake-ninja module; Ants source enters via type: dir / path: ../.. for local builds and re-points to type: git / url / tag for Flathub submission.

Added — Flatpak host-shell wiring in ptyhandler.cpp: the forked child probes FLATPAK_ID and /.flatpak-info (either signal triggers the branch) and exec's the user's shell via flatpak-spawn --host with --env= forwarding for TERM / COLORTERM / TERM_PROGRAM / TERM_PROGRAM_VERSION (pulled from the ANTS_VERSION compile definition, not a literal) / COLORFGBG, plus --directory=<workDir> gated on !workDir.isEmpty(). The terminal emulator itself (VT parser, renderer, plugin VM) stays inside the sandbox; only the child shell escapes so tools and $HOME are reachable. Same PTY model as Ghostty's Flathub build. Outside Flatpak the existing execlp path runs byte-for-byte unchanged.

Added — packaging/flatpak/README.md: local build (flatpak-builder --install --user), host-shell rationale, Flathub submission workflow, and the documented Lua-plugins-disabled limitation for the initial manifest (plugin support returns once a shared-modules Lua entry is wired up).

Added — packaging/README.md Flatpak section: layout tree adds flatpak/; "Flatpak — org.ants.Terminal.yml" section mirrors the openSUSE / Arch / Debian sections; shared-concerns header updated from three recipes to four.

Added — feature test tests/features/flatpak_host_shell/: source-grep regression test pinning the branch shape — detection probes both signals in an OR, --host and -- separators appear in argv, every TERM var crosses via --env=, workDir gates on isEmpty(), direct-exec fallback is preserved verbatim, _exit(127) is the post-exec fall-through on execvp failure.
Changed — ROADMAP.md H6 flipped from 📋 to ✅ manifest (0.7.2) + 📋 Flathub submission residual.

description.p: First of the 0.8.0 🎨 multiplexing roadmap items lands ahead of the larger mux-server / remote-control work: SSH ControlMaster auto-multiplexing from the bookmark dialog. A second tab to the same host now reuses the first tab's session, skipping the full auth handshake — same precedent as kitty's ssh kitten, Warp's SSH blocks, and iTerm2's SSH profiles.

ul.li: SSH ControlMaster auto-multiplexing: connects opened from the SSH Manager carry -o ControlMaster=auto, -o ControlPath=$HOME/.ssh/cm-%r@%h:%p, and -o ControlPersist=10m when the new ssh_control_master config key is true (default). Opt-out available for sites that forbid lingering multiplex sockets by policy. $HOME resolves in-process via QDir::homePath() so the ControlPath works under dash / POSIX sh; %r@%h:%p are OpenSSH ControlPath substitution tokens and survive shell quoting intact. Feature test ssh_control_master pins six invariants covering token presence/absence, $HOME resolution, positional ordering, and coexistence with the legacy -p / -i / extraArgs flags.

description.p: Shell integration + triggers — the 0.7.0 minor release rolls up the 0.6.1 → 0.6.45 patch-release arc into the theme the 0.7 ROADMAP plotted out. Command blocks become first-class UI, .cast recording ships, semantic history turns path:line:col into Ctrl-click jumps, OSC 133 markers gain HMAC verification, the full iTerm2-parity trigger set lands, the VT parser goes SIMD with a dedicated PTY/parse worker thread, Quake mode runs Wayland-native via layer-shell + GlobalShortcuts portal, and the H1–H4 distribution slice (SECURITY.md, AppStream metainfo, man page, shell completions) takes Ants from "side project" to "distro-ready." No breaking API changes — every plugin that loaded against 0.6.45 loads against 0.7.0.

ul.li: Shell integration: command blocks as first-class UI with OSC 133 prompt/command/output grouping, Ctrl+Shift+Up / Down jumps, collapsible output with "… N lines hidden" summaries, a 2px pass/fail stripe, and per-block right-click menu (Copy Command, Copy Output, Re-run, Fold/Unfold, Share as .cast). Asciinema .cast v2 recording — full-session via Ctrl+Shift+R or per-block via the context menu.

Semantic history: Ctrl-click on a path:line:col capture in scrollback opens the file at the cited position. CWD resolution via /proc/<pid>/cwd so relative paths Just Work. Editor support covers VS Code family, vi-family, nano, Sublime, Helix, Micro, and JetBrains.

Security: shell-side HMAC verification for OSC 133 markers — when $ANTS_OSC133_KEY is set, forged markers are dropped and a status-bar counter surfaces the count. Plugin capability audit UI renders every declared permission as a revocable checkbox in Settings → Plugins. Image-bomb defenses cap total decoded image bytes at 256 MB per terminal across Sixel / Kitty / iTerm2 OSC 1337.

Triggers + plugin events: full iTerm2-parity trigger rule set (bell, inject, run_script, notify, sound, command, highlight_line, highlight_text, make_hyperlink) with an instant flag. OSC 1337 SetUserVar with user_var_changed event. Command-palette plugin registration via ants.palette.register(). New events: command_finished, pane_focused, theme_changed, window_config_reloaded, user_var_changed, palette_action.

Performance: SIMD VT-parser scan — ground-state hot path scans 16 bytes at a time via SSE2 (x86_64) / NEON (ARM64). Dedicated PTY read + VT parse worker thread decouples input from the GUI with bounded back-pressure (≤ 8 batches in flight). Incremental reflow on resize skips the allocation-heavy rewrap round-trip for standalone lines.

Platform — Wayland native: layer-shell Quake mode promotes the window to zwlr_layer_surface_v1 at LayerTop when layer-shell-qt6-devel is present (XCB path preserved for X11). Freedesktop GlobalShortcuts portal wires the toggle-quake id on KDE Plasma 6 and xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland / -wlr; in-app QShortcut fallback stays active on GNOME Shell.

Distribution readiness (H1–H4): SECURITY.md + CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (coordinated-disclosure policy, Contributor Covenant 2.1) clear the Debian / Fedora / Ubuntu security-team review gate. AppStream metainfo + polished desktop entry with Desktop Actions for NewWindow + QuakeMode. groff -man man page at packaging/linux/ants-terminal.1. Shell completions for bash / zsh / fish installed to conventional vendor paths — auto-discovered on system-wide installs. CI runs appstreamcli validate --explain, desktop-file-validate, and groff -man -Tutf8 -wall on every push.

Dev experience: Project Audit gains four new rules (unbounded_callback_payloads, qnetworkreply_no_abort, silent_catch, missing_build_flags, no_ci), fixture-per-check self-consistency enforcement (audit_fixture_coverage), and a build-asan CI lane running ctest + binary smoke under ASan/UBSan on every push. CONTRIBUTING.md covers build modes, test layout, adding an audit rule, version-bump checklist. actions/checkout bumped v4.2.2 → v5.0.1 to pre-empt the 2026-06-02 Node 20 deprecation.

Notes: 0.7.0 is a minor-release rollup of 30 patch releases; the individual 0.6.x CHANGELOG entries remain authoritative for the SHAs they shipped under. Deferred items (EGL swap-with-damage on GL, Domain abstraction, persistent workspaces, Kitty Unicode-placeholder graphics) are explicitly re-scoped to 0.8.0+.

description.p: Deferred VT-standards items from the 10th-audit research report, plus atomic-write hardening for long-lived audit state. The 11th periodic audit returned 0 of ~103 actionable findings — the baseline stays clean. User-visible win: modern Go / Rust TUIs (delta, lazygit, fzf, jj, neovim, bat, most termenv-based tools) can now auto-detect Ants' dark-vs-light theme without the unreliable COLORFGBG env var, and tmux / neovim / kitty-kitten can save-and-restore terminal state around their own output.

ul.li: Added: OSC 10 / 11 / 12 colour queries. Apps that probe \e]10;?\e\\ etc. get the xterm 16-bit-per-channel reply \e]<n>;rgb:RRRR/GGGG/BBBB\e\\. Only the query form is honoured — the set form is deliberately silently dropped so default fg / bg / cursor colour stays theme-driven and in-terminal programs can't inject a theme.

Added: DECRQSS (Request Status String). DCS $q requests for DECSTBM (scroll-region, r), SGR (current attributes, m), and DECSCUSR (cursor shape, q) reply with DCS 1 $ r … per the xterm spec. Unknown settings reply "invalid" (DCS 0 $ r …) rather than dropping silently so callers fall back to defaults. The DECRQSS branch gates on the $q prefix before Sixel's q search, so Sixel image traffic still routes to the image handler.

Changed: atomic JSON writes via QSaveFile for four long-lived audit-state files — audit_rule_quality.json (frequent per-fire / per-suppression writes; corruption loses every rule score), .audit_cache/trend.json (cumulative severity totals), .audit_cache/baseline.json (fingerprints anchoring the "new findings only" filter), and the .audit_suppress v1 → v2 migration rewrite. Torn writes from crash or kill -9 can no longer corrupt any of these. The one-line .audit_suppress append path stays non-atomic on purpose — the loader already skips malformed lines.

Tests: new osc_color_query feature test locks the OSC 10/11/12 response shape plus the query-only gate (set form MUST NOT produce a response); new decrqss test locks the DECSTBM / SGR / DECSCUSR replies, the "invalid" reply bytes, and a Sixel-routing regression guard so the new DECRQSS branch can't eat image traffic.

description.p: Project Audit dialog self-review pass. The 10th periodic audit returned 0 of 55 actionable findings — the nature of the 55 pointed at three bits of friction the dialog was creating for itself: it was auditing its own output, every "is this noise?" verdict needed an individual click, and users had no summary-level readout for "how much of this run was signal?". All three are closed in this release.

ul.li: Added: signal-ratio readout in the summary banner. renderResults() now prints an "X% actionable" figure — signal / (signal + noise) where noise = suppressions + AI-verdict FALSE_POSITIVE — below the severity pills with colour coding (green ≥60%, amber 20–59%, red <20%). A 30-day rolling average pulled from RuleQualityTracker sits next to it as a peer comparison ("30d avg X% actionable, n=Y fires").

Added: batch AI triage. A 🧠 Triage visible (N) button next to the confidence-sort toggle sends every visible, not-yet-triaged finding to the configured LLM in a single JSON request (hard cap 20 per batch; above that it slices and dispatches sequentially). Responses are spliced back by dedup key so a reordering model can't misalign results. Prompt-injection hardening inherited from the single-finding path. Hidden entirely when AI isn't configured.

Changed: isGeneratedFile() now auto-skips prior audit artifacts — docs/AUTOMATED_AUDIT_REPORT_*.{json,md,html,txt} (SHA-256 dedup keys that gitleaks flagged as high-entropy secrets), .audit_cache/ and audit_rule_quality.json (the dialog's own baseline + self-learning ledger; auditing them is circular), and tests/audit_fixtures/** (bad.* fixtures intentionally embed strings third-party scanners misread as real findings). No project configuration needed.

Notes: no schema changes. audit_rule_quality.json and audit_rules.json are read-compatible with prior versions. The 30-day actionable% uses fires as the denominator rather than run count, because run count isn't directly recorded — the label makes the denominator explicit (n=<count> fires).

description.p: Two user-reported regressions closed, plus a quality-of-life default flip. The 0.6.42 focus-redirect guards didn't cure the menubar hover flash — the real culprit was a stylesheet gap, not focus churn. And users coming back to the app expected their tabs to be waiting for them; session persistence now ships default-on, matching every modern terminal (iTerm2, Kitty, WezTerm, Konsole).

ul.li: Fixed: menubar hover no longer flashes. The 0.6.42 pass narrowed focus-redirect firing but didn't address the root cause. The stylesheet defined QMenuBar::item:selected without a matching base QMenuBar::item rule, so Qt fell back to the native style for the non-selected state and composited native drawing under the :selected overlay. Added an explicit transparent-background QMenuBar::item base rule plus a :pressed rule so every menubar item state is owned entirely by the stylesheet; the 0.6.42 focus guards remain as defense-in-depth.

Fixed: tabs are now remembered between sessions by default. Config::sessionPersistence() defaulted to false, so users who never toggled Settings → General → "Save and restore sessions" lost their tab set on every quit. Flipped the default to true; users with session_persistence: false explicit in config.json still get their opt-out honoured — only the absent-key path reads the new fallback. The 5-second uptime floor in saveAllSessions() still protects against crash-on-launch wiping real state.
Changed: Config::sessionPersistence() default flipped from false to true. First-run users now get tab restore out of the box; explicit values in config.json are respected unchanged.

Tests: new tests/features/session_persistence_default/ three-invariant feature test pins the truth table — missing key → true, explicit true → true, explicit false → false. Linked against src/config.cpp only (no GUI dependency). Brings the feature-test count to 25 and the total ctest count to 26.

Internal: cleaned up a -Wshadow warning at mainwindow.cpp:3921 (Review-Changes finalizer lambda's const Theme &th shadowed the outer th; renamed to lth) and dropped an unused <cstdlib> include from the new test.

description.p: Focus-redirect guard rails. Two user-visible regressions from the 0.6.26 auto-return-to-terminal behaviour — a flashing highlight as the mouse crossed the menubar, and the paste-confirmation dialog's "Paste" button silently swallowing mouse clicks — both traced back to the same QApplication::focusChanged handler firing through narrow windows it shouldn't. New guards short-circuit the redirect when a popup is open, when the cursor is over a menu or menubar, or when any top-level QDialog is visible — the last covers the QMessageBox::exec() modality-handshake tick during which activeModalWidget() can briefly be null.

ul.li: Fixed: menubar File / Edit / View hover no longer flickers. The focus-redirect lambda now early-returns when QApplication::activePopupWidget() is non-null and when QApplication::widgetAt(QCursor::pos()) has a QMenu or QMenuBar ancestor. Both guards are mirrored in the deferred QTimer::singleShot(0,…) fire-time block so a menu opened between queue and fire is still honoured.

Fixed: the paste-confirmation QMessageBox no longer swallows clicks on the "Paste" button (pre-0.6.42 only the &Paste mnemonic worked). The redirect now walks QApplication::topLevelWidgets() at queue time and early-returns on any visible QDialog, mirroring the fire-time check and covering the brief show() handshake window where activeModalWidget() returns null.

Tests: new tests/features/focus_redirect_menu_guard/ source-grep pins six invariants on the focusChanged lambda in src/mainwindow.cpp — the popup and menu-hover guards (queue + fire time), the visible-dialog walk, and the five pre-existing guards (activeModalWidget, QAbstractButton, mouseButtons, QDialog parent chain, CommandPalette) that must not regress. Uses the balanced-brace scanner from 0.6.41 to avoid std::regex stack blow-out on a 4000+ line file.

description.p: Quake-mode + OSC 133 UX polish. Surfaces the out-of-focus portal binding state in Settings so users can tell at a glance whether their Quake hotkey escapes focus, and adds a scrollbar-adjacent tick-mark gutter that exposes OSC 133 command boundaries across the full scrollback — jump targets for Ctrl+Shift+Up/Down that were previously invisible.

ul.li: Added: command-mark gutter (show_command_marks, default on) — a 4-px vertical strip just left of the scrollbar draws one tick per PromptRegion, color-coded by last exit status: green (success), red (non-zero exit), gray (in-progress, OSC 133 D not yet emitted). Positions compress linearly so a 50,000-line scrollback stays fully visible in the widget height.

Added: Settings → Terminal → Scrollback toggle "Show command markers in scrollbar gutter" (default on) with a tooltip explaining the color states. Propagates to all live terminals via applyConfigToTerminal — no restart needed.

Added: portal-binding status label in Settings → Quake. One line under the hotkey field reports either "Portal binding active" (green, hotkey works when Ants is unfocused — KDE Plasma 6, Hyprland, wlroots) or "Portal unavailable" (amber, hotkey works only while focused), based on GlobalShortcutsPortal::isAvailable() at dialog construction.

Invariant: gutter drawing gates on m_showCommandMarks AND non-empty promptRegions(), so users without shell integration see nothing change; ticks never overlap the scrollbar thumb (offset by m_scrollBar->width()).

description.p: OSC 133 shell-integration polish. Adds two keyboard-driven "last completed command" actions that complement the existing block-at-cursor right-click menu with no-selection-needed top-level equivalents — the iTerm2 ⇧⌘O / WezTerm CopyLastOutput convention, surfaced through the View menu, the command palette, and configurable keybindings.

ul.li: Added: TerminalWidget::copyLastCommandOutput() slot — walks promptRegions() backwards for the most recent region with commandEndMs > 0 && hasOutput and copies the full output via outputTextAt(idx) (unbounded). Skips in-progress tails so Ctrl+Alt+O during sleep 30 copies the previous command's output, never the partial in-progress one.
Added: TerminalWidget::rerunLastCommand() slot — same backwards-walk / skip-in-progress rule, then writes the command text + \r to the PTY via rerunCommandAt(idx).

Added: View menu entries "Copy Last Command Output" (default Ctrl+Alt+O, config key copy_last_output) and "Re-run Last Command" (default Ctrl+Alt+R, config key rerun_last_command). Both surface automatically in the command palette; Ctrl+Alt+* avoids the already-taken Ctrl+Shift+O (split pane) and Ctrl+Shift+R (record session).

Added: status-bar toasts for discoverability — success path shows "Copied N chars of last command output"; failure path shows "No completed command to copy (enable shell integration)", a load-bearing hint that OSC 133 needs bash/zsh/fish shell-integration hooks.

Invariant: locked by tests/features/osc133_last_command (CTest labels features;fast, part of the release gate) — pins the backwards-walk, the outputTextAt-not-lastCommandOutput choice, the keybinding wiring, and the toast contract.

description.p: Wayland-native Quake-mode, part 2 of 2 — completes the 0.7.0 roadmap item. When the xdg-desktop-portal service is available on the session bus, Ants Terminal now registers its Quake toggle with the Freedesktop Portal org.freedesktop.portal.GlobalShortcuts API (KDE Plasma 6, xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland, xdg-desktop-portal-wlr). The hotkey fires whether or not Ants has focus. On GNOME Shell (portal service present but no GlobalShortcuts backend yet) and on minimal desktops without a portal, the in-app QShortcut fallback from 0.6.38 stays as the activation path — no regression.

ul.li: Added: GlobalShortcutsPortal client class wrapping the three-step D-Bus handshake (CreateSession → session handle → BindShortcuts → Activated) behind a single Qt signal. Handles request-path prediction, custom meta-type registration for the a(sa{sv}) shortcuts array, and session lifecycle.
Added: portal-aware Quake setup in MainWindow — when the portal is available, binds the toggle-quake id with config.quake_hotkey as the preferred trigger.
Added: focused double-fire debounce between the in-app QShortcut and the portal Activated paths (500 ms window) so a focused system doesn't hide-then-show the window in one frame.

Added: qtKeySequenceToPortalTrigger helper that converts Qt's "Ctrl+Shift+`" into the freedesktop shortcut-syntax "CTRL+SHIFT+grave". Deliberately minimal — modifiers plus a handful of common punctuation; unrecognised keys pass through.

Invariant: new tests/features/global_shortcuts_portal/ regression test pins (1) isAvailable() gate on portal construction; (2) canonical D-Bus service / path / interface / method literals; (3) bound id "toggle-quake" matches the Activated handler filter; (4) in-app QShortcut fallback stays wired; (5) both activation paths debounce via m_lastQuakeToggleMs; (6) CMakeLists.txt lists the source + keeps Qt6::DBus linked; (7) header surfaces the expected public API.

description.p: Wayland-native Quake-mode, part 1 of 2. When layer-shell-qt6 is present at build time, Ants Terminal's drop-down Quake mode now promotes its window to a zwlr_layer_surface_v1 at the top layer, anchored to the top edge of the active screen — the Wayland equivalent of the X11 _NET_WM_STATE_ABOVE + client-side move() the XCB path has used all along. Also fixes a long-standing dead-code bug: the quake_hotkey config key was saved from Settings but never wired to a shortcut; the QLineEdit value is now honoured in-app. True out-of-focus global hotkey registration (via the Freedesktop Portal GlobalShortcuts API) is the 0.6.39 follow-up.

ul.li: Added: LayerShellQt integration — CMakeLists.txt does find_package(LayerShellQt CONFIG QUIET) and defines ANTS_WAYLAND_LAYER_SHELL when found. Missing = Wayland Quake falls back to the Qt toplevel path; X11 Quake is unaffected.
Fixed: the quake_hotkey config key is now wired to a QShortcut invoking toggleQuakeVisibility(). Pre-0.6.38 the Settings dialog saved the user's chosen sequence but nothing consumed it.

Fixed: the slide-up/slide-down animation no longer snaps on Wayland — QPropertyAnimation on pos() is a no-op there (the compositor owns position), so the Wayland path uses a plain show/hide toggle. XCB slide animation is unchanged.
Packaging: openSUSE spec adds BuildRequires: cmake(LayerShellQt); Arch PKGBUILD adds layer-shell-qt to makedepends + optdepends; Debian control adds liblayershellqt6-dev to Build-Depends.
