Invariant: new tests/features/wayland_quake_mode/ regression test pins (1) the layer-shell include is guarded by ANTS_WAYLAND_LAYER_SHELL; (2) setupQuakeMode() dispatches X11 vs Wayland at runtime; (3) toggleQuakeVisibility() skips the pos() animation on Wayland; (4) the quake_hotkey → QShortcut wire exists; (5) CMake uses the QUIET flavour of find_package.

description.p: Phase 3 of the 0.7.0 threading refactor. The ANTS_SINGLE_THREADED=1 kill-switch introduced in 0.6.34 is retired, and the legacy single-threaded read/parse code paths are deleted from TerminalWidget. The bake period (0.6.34 → 0.6.37) proved the worker path; the 0.6.35 hotfix proved the kill-switch worked. Every launch now goes through VtStream on the worker thread — one path, one set of invariants, four feature tests.

ul.li: Removed: the ANTS_SINGLE_THREADED=1 environment kill-switch and the legacy TerminalWidget::startShell() branch it guarded.
Removed: legacy m_pty / m_parser members and the onPtyData slot — the widget no longer owns a Pty or VtParser directly; both live inside VtStream on the worker thread.
Changed: hasPty() and ptyChildPid() simplified to worker-only checks; no behaviour change at the call sites.

description.p: Tenth periodic audit. Two actionable findings across ~110 raw — a 2% signal ratio consistent with the codebase's audit-calibration anchor. Both fixed in a single pass.

ul.li: Fixed: RuleQualityTracker::prune() was dead code — save() inlined the same retention logic. Removed the dead function; save() is now the single source of truth for the 90-day cutoff.
Fixed: pipeRun regex in paste-confirmation was recompiled on every paste. Hoisted to static const so the pattern compiles once per process.

description.p: Hotfix for the 0.6.34 threaded-parse refactor. Keystrokes typed into the terminal were silently dropped — the echo never appeared on screen. Twelve PTY-write call sites in TerminalWidget (keystrokes, arrow keys, paste, focus reporting, scratchpad, context menu, and more) still carried pre-0.6.34 if (m_pty) ptyWrite(...) guards. Under the threaded parse path m_pty is null (the Pty now lives on the worker thread inside VtStream), so the guard silently swallowed every write. Replaced each with either hasPty() (path-agnostic) or an unconditional ptyWrite(...) call (the helper is null-safe). A new threaded_ptywrite_gating feature test pins the invariant at the source level so the bug can't come back quietly.

ul.li: Fixed: keystrokes and paste no longer silently drop on the threaded parse path.
Fixed: shellPid() / shellCwd() / foregroundProcess() now work on the worker path via a new ptyChildPid() helper.
Added: threaded_ptywrite_gating regression test — source-level guard against stale m_pty null-checks outside the allowlisted legacy-path functions.

description.p: Decouples PTY read and VT parsing from the Qt GUI thread. Previously, every byte of terminal output traversed the GUI thread through QSocketNotifier → Pty::dataReceived → VtParser::feed → TerminalGrid::processAction and then on to paint — so heavy streams (find /, cat biglog) blocked scroll and repaint. As of 0.6.34 read + parse run on a dedicated worker QThread (VtStream); only batched VtAction streams cross back to GUI for grid application and paint. Ships the first 0.7.0 performance item early since the architectural change landed clean with three feature-conformance tests locking the invariants.

ul.li: Added: VtStream worker wrapper (src/vtstream.{h,cpp}) — owns Pty and VtParser on a dedicated QThread, accumulates parser output into VtBatch and ships it to GUI over Qt::QueuedConnection. Grid and paint path stay on GUI so the refactor surface stays small.

Added: back-pressure — worker buffers up to 8 batches (~128 KB of unprocessed PTY bytes) before disabling its read notifier so the kernel applies flow control to the child; GUI re-enables reads on drain. Replaces "let RAM grow unbounded while GUI catches up" with kernel-natural flow control.
Added: Pty::setReadEnabled(bool) for the back-pressure path. Pty::write and Pty::resize are now slots so they can be invoked cross-thread via QMetaObject::invokeMethod.

Added: three feature-conformance tests under tests/features/ — threaded_parse_equivalence (11 fixtures × 6 chunking strategies, pins action-stream identity), threaded_response_ordering (DA1/CPR/DA2/mode-996/DSR-OK order across 3 feed strategies), and threaded_resize_synchronous (source-level contract that invokeMethod uses Qt::BlockingQueuedConnection).

Changed: all PTY-write call sites in TerminalWidget route through a new ptyWrite() helper — keystrokes, bracketed paste, Kitty kbd protocol, response callbacks, snippets, scratchpad. The helper branches on worker-active vs. legacy path; correctness is centralised.
Changed: resize path uses Qt::BlockingQueuedConnection so the PTY winsize is updated before resizeEvent returns — no 1-frame flicker at the new grid size with old PTY dims.

Changed: session logging and asciicast timestamps are now sampled on the worker at batch-flush time rather than on GUI at onPtyData time; asciicast recordings are more accurate under paint load. Log write granularity is per-batch rather than per-PTY-read (coarser but still monotonic).

description.p: Kill switch: set ANTS_SINGLE_THREADED=1 in the environment to force the legacy single-threaded path while the new code proves itself. It will be removed in a follow-up release once no regressions surface.

Nine long-standing user-reported bugs around the status bar, scrollback integrity, session restore, and the Review Changes dialog — fixed in one pass. The quiet centerpiece is the root-cause diagnosis of the long tail of "Review Changes button does nothing" reports: a 0.6.26-era focusChanged lambda was firing a singleShot(0) refocus between a button's mousePress and mouseRelease, ripping focus off the button mid-click and silently cancelling the clicked() signal. Also retires the ElidedLabel + stylesheet-chip-padding class of bug by converting every fixed-width status-bar chip to plain QLabel / QPushButton with QSizePolicy::Fixed.

ul.li: Added: one-click "Install Claude Code status-bar hooks" action in Settings > General — writes ~/.config/ants-terminal/hooks/claude-forward.sh and idempotently merges PreToolUse / PostToolUse / Stop / PreCompact / SessionStart entries into ~/.claude/settings.json, preserving any existing hooks.

Added: frozen-view scrollback snapshot — while scrolled up, cellAtGlobal / combiningAt read from a snapshot of the screen at scroll-up time, not the live grid, so the viewport is completely immune to live screen writes. Locked by tests/features/scrollback_frozen_view/.

Added: richer Claude status vocabulary (idle / thinking / bash / reading / editing / searching / browsing / planning / auditing / prompting / compacting) with distinct theme colours; plan-mode and /audit detection in the transcript parser; new planModeChanged and auditingChanged signals; notification_timeout_ms and tab_color_sequence config keys.

Changed: Review Changes button is now tri-state — hidden when not a git repo, disabled when clean and in-sync, enabled otherwise. Probe switched to git status --porcelain=v1 -b so unpushed commits count as reviewable work. The global QPushButton:hover rule is now :enabled-gated. The dialog is fully async — opens instantly with a placeholder and streams git output in, with the status-bar button disabled while it is open.

Changed: status-bar layout uses QSizePolicy::Fixed across the branch / process / Claude-status / button chips. Only the transient notification slot is elastic and elides. Branch chip colour moved from green to cyan so it is visually distinct from Claude-state chips.

Fixed: the focus-redirect race — the focusChanged lambda now exempts QAbstractButton focus targets and defers while a mouse button is held down, so button clicks on status-bar chrome are no longer cancelled mid-press.

Fixed: scrollback blank-line bloat on inactive tabs after session restart. Two root causes: recalcGridSize ran on unlaid-out tabs and shrank grids to 3×10 (pre-layout guard added); TerminalGrid::resize pushed every reflowed row to scrollback (now skips entirely-blank rows).
Fixed: tab colours not persisting across restarts — UUID-keyed tab_groups only worked with session persistence enabled; an ordered tab_color_sequence fallback is now applied at startup.
Fixed: Add-to-Allowlist button gains a 3-scan debounce against TUI-repaint flicker and a shared toolFinished / sessionStopped retraction path across scroll-scan and hook detections.

description.p: Audit self-learning layer + contributor-facing docs + a one-line CI hotfix for 0.6.31's OSC 133 verifier. The headline is a per-rule fire/suppression tracker that records every audit finding and user suppression to <project>/audit_rule_quality.json, plus a new "📊 Rule Quality" button in the audit dialog that surfaces the noisiest rules first. A longest-common-substring suggester turns the last few suppressions for a noisy rule into a one-line dropIfContains proposal that's a copy-paste away from the user's audit_rules.json override pack.

ul.li: Added — audit self-learning: RuleQualityTracker (src/auditrulequality.{h,cpp}) records per-rule fire and suppression events with a 90-day rolling window and 50 000-record-per-category cap. Saves on dialog destruction (RAII) and force-flushes on each suppression so user actions are durable.

Added — Rule Quality dialog: new modal table view listing per-rule 30-day fires, 30-day suppressions, FP rate (capped 0-100 %), all-time fires, and a proposed dropIfContains tightening when the heuristic suggester has enough samples. Sorted by FP rate desc so the noisiest rules surface first.

Added — LCS tightening suggester: longest common substring across the last 5 suppressed line texts per rule. Floor at 6 chars + at least one structural boundary character (space, paren, brace, bracket, semicolon, quote, angle bracket) so we propose rule-shape filters, not project-noun filters. Surfaces inline in the status bar on every suppression.

Added — docs: docs/RECOMMENDED_ROUTINES.md captures the four Claude Code routines that earn a slot on the shared account quota for this repo — nightly audit triage, PR security review, weekly audit-rule self-triage (the deeper LLM-driven counterpart to the always-on tracker), and a monthly ROADMAP-item scoper.

Fixed — CI build: 0.6.31's OSC 133 verifier used a QByteArrayView overload of QMessageAuthenticationCode::addData not available on the Ubuntu runner's Qt 6.x. Switched to the (const char*, qsizetype) overload present everywhere; behaviour unchanged.

description.p: Security + reliability release. The headline is OSC 133 HMAC-signed shell integration: when $ANTS_OSC133_KEY is set, every prompt / command / exit marker must carry a matching HMAC-SHA256 tag, and forged markers from in-terminal processes are dropped (with a throttled status-bar warning). When the key is unset, behaviour is unchanged from 0.6.30 — no upgrade penalty for users who don't opt in.

ul.li: Security: OSC 133 HMAC verification closes the spoofing surface where any process writing to stdout (a malicious TUI, cat malicious.txt) could otherwise mint shell-integration markers and pollute prompt regions, exit codes, and the command_finished plugin event. Shell hooks ship under ${datarootdir}/ants-terminal/shell-integration/ for bash and zsh.

Fixed: Review Changes status-bar button no longer silently swallows clicks on a clean repo — the disabled-but-visible state was advertising itself as actionable via the global hover stylesheet while never emitting clicked(). The button is now hidden on clean repos and reappears the moment a real diff exists.

Fixed: Shift+Enter no longer wedges the terminal tab in bracketed-paste mode (0.6.26 regression). A QByteArray(literal, 8) truncation dropped the closing [201~ end-paste marker; switched to QByteArrayLiteral so the size is derived at compile time.

Fixed: Status-bar Add-to-allowlist button no longer stacks duplicates when a scroll-scan detection fires while a hook-path container is already visible — dedup lookup now uses QWidget* for parity with the other lookup sites.

Fixed — audit signal-to-noise: Two-pass triage of the Ants and Vestige audit runs cuts the false-positive rate from ~97% to under 10% with no loss of real coverage. Tightened memory_patterns, secrets_scan, file_perms, and header_guards rules; dropped clazy qt-keywords; collapsed clang-tidy driver-error storms; added context-window suppressions to six rules; demoted tool timeouts and advisory checks to Info severity.

description.p: Contributor-facing workflow infrastructure release. No runtime or behavior changes for end users — the binary is bit-identical to 0.6.29 modulo the version string. New files are tooling for contributors who clone the repo and work on it through Claude Code.

ul.li: New .claude/settings.json wires a PostToolUse hook on Edit|Write that delegates to packaging/hook-on-cmakelists-edit.sh. The script runs check-version-drift.sh the moment an agent edits CMakeLists.txt and surfaces drift via systemMessage — closes the honor-system gap that allowed the 0.6.23 packaging slip.
New .claude/bump.json recipe consumed by a user-level /bump skill. Lists the 6 version-bearing files with templated find/replace, plus 4 todos for content the recipe can't synthesise.
New packaging/hook-on-cmakelists-edit.sh: small bash dispatcher; stdin is PostToolUse JSON, stdout is empty on clean state or a systemMessage on drift; always exits 0.

.gitignore narrowed .claude/ → .claude/* with explicit exceptions for settings.json and bump.json so the team-shared workflow files commit while developer-local settings.local.json and worktrees/ stay ignored.

description.p: Status-bar reliability pass — nail down every user-reported symptom in one go: git branch chip "…" under tight layout, Review Changes button not appearing until a tab-switch, Add-to-Allowlist button silently no-op on save failure, Claude status label 2 s stale after tab-switch.

ul.li: ElidedLabel::minimumSizeHint now returns the full-text width (capped at maximumWidth). Short text is guaranteed to render without elision regardless of layout squeeze — the statusbar's QBoxLayout previously compressed chips to a 3-char minimum and the text came out as "…" even with ample space. sizeHint also no longer reflects post-elision text (avoiding the chicken-and-egg loop where elision fed its own minimum).

Review Changes button is now refreshed on every 2 s status-bar tick (not only tab-switch + Claude hook fileChanged). Dirty repos on startup show the button immediately; manual git add / git commit / external editor workflows flip the button live without needing Claude hooks.

ClaudeIntegration::setShellPid calls pollClaudeProcess() immediately on PID change so the status label reflects the new tab within one event-loop iteration rather than being NotRunning for up to 2 s until the next timer tick.

ClaudeAllowlistDialog::saveSettings now returns bool; all three callers (prompt-prefill, OK, Apply) surface failure to the user via status message or validation label. Previously every open/write/commit failure was swallowed and the button appeared to do nothing.

Add-to-Allowlist button is now prompt-scoped in both creation paths: the hook-server button-group QWidget has objectName "claudeAllowBtn" matching the scroll-scan path, so onTabChanged cleans up both; the hook-server path's cleared-listener covers every terminal (not just currentTerminal()) so approving on the originating tab after a brief visit to another still clears the button.

showDiffViewer replaces every silent return with an explicit status message so the Review Changes button can't click through to nothing — previously a failed focusedTerminal, empty shellCwd, or non-zero git exit all disappeared without feedback. Combined git diff HEAD in a single invocation (covers worktree + index) rather than two.
New tests/features/status_bar_elision feature test locks the short-text-never-elides invariant inside a real QMainWindow + QStatusBar harness; fails against pre-fix ElidedLabel, passes on fix.

description.p: End-to-end feature review — same-class overflow sweep across the status bar, title bar, and audit dialog; quake-mode toggle fix; combining-character preservation on resize; disk-write durability hardening; auto-profile regex caching.

ul.li: New ElidedLabel widget truncates long text with "…" to a bounded width. Applied to the three status-bar text slots (git branch, transient message, foreground process), the window title label, the Claude status label, and the audit-dialog status label — each previously grew unbounded and pushed siblings off-screen.

Quake-mode toggle no longer auto-hides the window 200 ms after a show toggle. The reused QPropertyAnimation's finished→hide() slot from a prior hide toggle was still connected on the show branch; the slot is now disconnected before every start() and re-attached only on the hide branch.

Terminal resize preserves the per-line combining-character side table on both main and alt screens. Pre-0.6.28 every resize wiped accents, ZWJ sequences, and diacritics because the simple-copy path walked cells only. Shrink cases cleanly evict combining entries whose column falls outside the new width. New tests/features/combining_on_resize feature test pins the three invariants.
Config and session writes now fsync() the temp file before atomic rename, closing the kernel-crash data-loss window on ext4 data=ordered.

Auto-profile rule regex is cached across the 2 s poll tick instead of recompiled per-rule-per-tick, and invalid patterns are detected via QRegularExpression::isValid() and warned once rather than silently never matching.

description.p: Seventh release in the 0.6 UX polish pass — ncurses clear scrollback fix, scroll-offset clamp, tab-close visibility, Claude "prompting" state.

ul.li: ncurses clear now correctly wipes scrollback on TERM=xterm-256color. The 0.6.26 Ink-overflow-repaint heuristic was false-matching the byte-burst because clear's \E[H\E[2J\E[3J sequence overlaps with Ink's clearTerminal. Disambiguated via cursor position at 3J time — clear emits H first so the cursor sits at (0,0); Ink emits H last so the cursor is at the bottom.
Scroll-to-bottom button no longer lingers when scrollback shrinks. m_scrollOffset is now re-clamped in the single updateScrollBar() choke-point after every output batch.
Tab close (×) button is always visible — removed the stylesheet rule that hid the glyph and left only an invisible hover hit-target.

Claude status bar now shows "Claude: prompting" when a permission prompt is waiting, even if the transcript-driven state still reports idle/thinking. Critical when the user is scrolled up in history and can't see the live prompt.

description.p: UX polish pass — status bar consistency, focus handling, Ink overflow-repaint fix, and tab colour gradient.

ul.li: Claude Code (Ink/React TUI) overflow-repaint no longer destroys scrollback or duplicates the replay. CSI 2J followed within 50 ms by CSI 3J on the main screen is now treated as Ink's clearTerminal frame-reset marker (see ink/build/ink.js:705) rather than a user-initiated scrollback wipe — scrollback is preserved and the suppression window is armed for the subsequent replay. Standalone CSI 3J (user-initiated clear -x) still wipes scrollback as expected. Two new feature-conformance scenarios assert both behaviours.

Status bar is now height-stable. QStatusBar's size hint had been tracking its tallest child, so the bar grew when the transient "Add to allowlist" button appeared and shrank when it went away. A 32 px minimum-height floor keeps the bar consistent whether or not a button is attached.

"Review Changes" status-bar button now matches the sibling "Add to allowlist" button's enabled look — it inherits the global QPushButton style instead of its own custom stylesheet (the 0.6.26 "bold to fix Gruvbox contrast" attempt made lowercase letters squish into a small-caps look on Fusion + monospace). Disabled state gets its own visual language (dashed border, italic, muted fg) so a clean-repo state reads clearly as non-actionable.

Git branch chip now has a hard vertical divider separating it from the transient-status slot, visible on low-contrast themes where the chip's rounded-background treatment was indistinguishable from the status bar background.

Focus auto-returns to the active terminal whenever focus lands on chrome widgets (status bar buttons, tab bar) with no dialog open. Clicking a status bar button, answering a prompt, etc., no longer leaves the keyboard parked on the button.

Claude Code context-usage progress bar is hidden until the first real (non-zero) context percentage arrives. A fresh tab, or a tab where Claude was never started, no longer paints a persistent "0%" widget.

Tab-colour badge replaced with a vertical gradient (transparent top → chosen colour bottom). The active-tab 2 px accent underline is preserved by excluding the bottom two pixels from the gradient area.

description.p: User-reported scrollback regression fix plus packaging-rule hardening.

ul.li: Scrolled-up viewport no longer shifts when streaming content arrives. The 0.6.21 pause and 0.6.22/0.6.24 CSI-clear window both skipped scrollback pushes, which froze the scroll anchor and let screen-scroll mechanics bleed into the user's viewport. Fix: push to scrollback while the user is scrolled up (anchor adjusts m_scrollOffset to compensate); the doubling-protection window still fires for the at-bottom case where the doubling symptom is observable.

Extended the packaging-version-drift audit rule to scan org.ants.Terminal.metainfo.xml. The file's <release version=> entry is version-coupled to every release but was not previously checked. Flagged by the ninth periodic audit (2026-04-15).

Extracted the drift-check logic into packaging/check-version-drift.sh — a standalone script that is the single source of truth. Both src/auditdialog.cpp and the CI workflow invoke the same script, so they can no longer drift apart.
Wired the script into .github/workflows/ci.yml as a gating check; a PR that bumps CMakeLists.txt without updating every packaging file now fails CI instead of merging and requiring a follow-up tag.

description.p: Status-bar button-lifecycle and legibility fixes alongside the full-clear scrollback suppression extension.

ul.li: Status-bar "Add to allowlist" button now persists for the full life of the Claude Code permission prompt — previously the button was retracted on the next repaint tick, vanishing within seconds of the prompt appearing.

"Review Changes" button disabled-state text now readable on every theme. Previous textSecondary-based fix still failed WCAG AA on One Dark / Kanagawa; switched to textPrimary + italic + dissolved border so the label stays legible while still communicating the disabled state.

Extended the 0.6.22 suppression window to CSI H; CSI 0J and corner-cursor CSI 1J — both produce the same post-state as CSI 2J and therefore exhibit the same doubling bug on TUI redraw bursts. Regression tests for both shapes added to tests/features/scrollback_redraw/.

Packaging recipes (openSUSE spec, Arch PKGBUILD, Debian changelog, man page) bumped to 0.6.24 after drifting two releases behind — the exact drift the packaging-version-drift audit rule was written to catch.

description.p: Three user-reported UX fixes, an event-driven status-bar contract, and the first 0.7.0 performance item — SIMD VT-parser scan.

ul.li: Allowlist "already covered" check no longer blocks legitimate compound rules — subsumption now splits on shell operators (&&, ||, ;, |) and requires every segment to match before declaring coverage.
Right-click tab colour picker now renders visibly — a new ColoredTabBar paints a 3-px strip outside stylesheet influence (was defeated by QSS specificity).
Status bar clears event-scoped transients (Allow buttons, notifications) on tab switch so state from one tab never leaks into another.
Fixed a Shift+Enter paste-image regression.
SIMD ground-state scan (SSE2 / NEON) — bulk Print emission on runs of safe printable-ASCII, with byte-identical action-stream regression guards.

description.p: Root-cause fix for main-screen TUI scrollback doubling, plus packaging catch-up across all recipes.

ul.li: Scrollback no longer accumulates duplicates when a main-screen TUI (Claude Code v2.1+) repaints via CSI 2J — a 250 ms sliding window now suppresses scrollback pushes during the repaint burst.
Packaging recipes (openSUSE spec, Arch PKGBUILD, Debian changelog) bumped to 0.6.22 after drifting three releases behind.
openSUSE spec gains %post/%postun scriptlets for desktop-database and icon-cache refresh.
Debian gains postinst/postrm scripts for the same purpose.

description.p: Audit-tool noise reduction, dialog theming, and a scrollback-insert pause for users actively reading history while a TUI repaints.

ul.li: Fixed a GNU grep 3.12 argument-ordering quirk that silently disabled the audit's --include file-type filter.
Extended the QSS cascade so every popup dialog inherits the terminal's active theme.
Added a scrollback-insert pause that stops TUI redraws from interleaving intermediate frames into the user's reading position.

description.p: Distribution-adoption bundle H5: OBS, AUR, and Debian packaging recipes.
Distribution-readiness bundle H4: bash, zsh, and fish shell completions plus CMake install rules.
Distribution-readiness bundle H3: ants-terminal(1) man page with CMake install rule.
Distribution-readiness bundle H2: AppStream metainfo and spec-compliant desktop entry.

ul.li: Added org.ants.Terminal.metainfo.xml for GNOME Software and KDE Discover discovery.
Added org.ants.Terminal.desktop following the reverse-DNS naming convention.
CMake install rules for desktop entry, metainfo, and hicolor-theme icons at six sizes.
CI validates packaging artefacts with appstreamcli and desktop-file-validate.

description.p: Distribution-readiness bundle H1: community and security policy documents.

ul.li: Added CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (Contributor Covenant 2.1) with dedicated reporting channels.
Changelogged the previously shipped SECURITY.md coordinated-disclosure policy.

description.p: Incremental scrollback reflow with a fast path for standalone lines.
Security hardening — URI scheme allowlist in addRowHyperlink.
